rulings.Case Title: INTERSTATE FIRE AND CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY VS. CLEVAND WRECKING COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Cleveland Wrecking Company'S Motion To Compel Deposition Testimony And Privilege Log Of Webcor Construction, Inc And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 13, 2011, Line 2, Defendant Cleveland Wrecking Company's Motion To Compel Deposition Testimony And Privilege Log Of Webcor Construction, Inc. And Request For Sanctions. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITION TESTIMONY OF MR. BRASCH IS DENIED. FIRST, MR. BRASCH'S TESTIMONY INDICATES THAT HIS DEALINGS WITH THE FRISKY LITIGATION WERE SOMEWHAT LIMITED AND TO THE EXTENT THAT HE PARTICIPATED IN THE TENDER MATTER AND DOES NOT RECALL SPECIFIC CONVERSATIONS, AN ORDER TO COMPEL WILL NOT REFRESH HIS RECOLLECTION. FURTHER, CLEVELAND CLAIMS TO HAVE A RECORD OF THE CONVERSATION AND WHAT WAS DISCUSSED. WEBCOR HAS ALSO AGREED TO PROVIDE PRIOR DEPOSITION TESTIMONY OF ITS EMPLOYEES INVOLVED IN THE FRISKY ACTION TO ASSIST CLEVELAND IN ITS QUEST TO DETERMINE LIABILITY FOR THE UNDERLYING INCIDENT. AS TO THE QUESTION TO MR. BRASCH REGARDING INTERPRETATION OF THE SUB-CONTRACT, THERE IS NO CLEAR NOTICE THAT THIS WOULD BE A MATTER FOR EXAMINATION AND IT IS HIGHLY QUESTIONABLE THAT HIS INTERPRETATION IS EVEN RELEVANT. AS TO THE REQUEST TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF PRIVILEGE LOGS, THAT ISSUE IS MOOT. REQUESTS FOR SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES PALK et al VS. MATTHEW GRAFFINGNA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Anti-Slapp Motion To Strike Pltf'S First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 13, 2011, Line 2, DEFENDANTS MATTHEW GRAFFINGNA, TIMOTHY GRAFFINGNA, MARK VASQUEZ, KRISTEN VASQUEZ, PETER MIKACICH Anti-Slapp Motion To Strike Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint. DEFENDANT'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE THE 7TH - 11TH CAUSES OF ACTION OF PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT IS DENIED. THE DEFENDANTS CONDUCT THAT IS THE SUBJECT OF PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT IS NOT CONSIDERED PROTECTED PRE-LITIGATION OR PETITIONING ACTIVITY. CLARK V. MAZGANI (2009) 170 CAL.APP.4TH 1281. PLAINTIFFS' REQUEST FOR FEES IN THE AMOUNT OF $4,125.00 IS GRANTED. GIVEN THAT THERE WAS CASE LAW DIRECTLY ON POINT, THE COURT DOES FIND THAT THIS MOTION IS FRIVOLOUS. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY, AS TRUSTEE VS. ERLINDA V. HERBERT et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 13, 2011, Line 5, PLAINTIFF DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY, AS TRUSTEE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AS TO WHETHER DEFENDANT, WHO IS NOT THE OWNER OF THE SUBJECT PROPERTY, CAN BE EVICTED. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO SHOW GOOD CAUSE. GROSS V. SUP. CRT. (1985) 171 CAL.APP.3RD 265. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: SAXON MORTGAGE SERVICES INC. VS. OSCR AVILA et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 13, 2011, line 6, DEFENDANT OSCAR AVILA'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. NOTICE TO CURE OR QUIT CONTAIN ERRONEOUS REFERENCE TO THE OAKLAND MUNICIPAL CODE. BECAUSE THESE MUST BE STRICTLY READ, THE COURT FINDS THE NOTICE DEFECTIVE. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ACTING BY AND VS. HAIGHT-ASHBURY NEIGHBORHOOD COUNCIL et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 13, 2011, Line 7, DEFENDANT HAIGHT-ASHBURY NEIGHBORHOOD COUNCIL'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT RE WHETHER THE PARTIES HAD A YEAR TO YEAR OR QUARTER TO QUARTER TERM AFTER EXPIRATION OF THE INITIAL 5 YEAR TERM. THERE IS ALSO A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AS TO WHETHER PLAINTIFF PROPERLY TERMINATED DEFENDANT'S TENANCY. =(302/LMG)
